campbell.title: Campbell City Council — February 3, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to approve a $4,000 donation from Water Tower Kitchen to help fund youth recreation scholarships. They also discussed extending a temporary local rule (Ordinance 2326) and moved forward with plans for a bicycle and pedestrian bridge over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue by applying for grant funding.
keyTopics: Water Tower Kitchen donating $4,000 for youth recreation scholarships
Extending temporary Ordinance 2326
Planning a new bicycle and pedestrian bridge over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue
Submitting grant applications for the Hamilton Avenue bridge project

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The City Council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026 to get feedback from residents about a proposed new rule or policy. After hearing from the public, the council moved forward by skipping the usual first step of introducing the ordinance and instead put it on the fast track for a vote at the next meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held to gather community input
New ordinance fast-tracked for adoption
Council waived standard first reading requirement
Vote scheduled for next regular council meeting

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 7, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 7, 2026, primarily to approve meeting minutes from various sessions held in March and to authorize the Town Manager to sign a construction contract with QLM, Inc. The agenda was mainly administrative housekeeping from the previous month's meetings.
keyTopics: Approval of March meeting minutes from multiple sessions
Construction contract authorization with QLM, Inc.
Review of closed session discussions from March

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 21, 2026
san-jose.summary: San José City Council met to approve several operational and infrastructure updates, including a contract renewal with the company that maintains baggage systems at the airport, new rules to protect affordable housing when subsidy programs end, and plans for updating the city's storm drain system. The council also discussed a family camp project funded by FEMA disaster assistance.
keyTopics: Airport baggage system contract renewal with Vanderlande Industries
New protections for affordable housing when federal subsidies expire
Storm sewer system upgrade planning and consultant agreement
FEMA-funded family camp project
Approval of previous committee meeting records

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 14, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The city council held a planning meeting to discuss Mountain View's spending plans for the next fiscal year, focusing on big projects like infrastructure improvements. They also officially recognized Teen Month and National Volunteer Week to celebrate young people and community volunteers in the city.
keyTopics: Next year's budget for building and fixing city infrastructure projects
Teen Month recognition and celebration
National Volunteer Week proclamation
Long-term city capital spending plans

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 7, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council met on April 7, 2026, to conduct regular city business and recognize two important observances: Arab-American Heritage Month and Fair Housing Month. The meeting began with a flag salute and special recognition of these community celebrations.
keyTopics: Arab-American Heritage Month recognition
Fair Housing Month observance
Regular City Council business

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 13, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council held a community workshop on April 13, 2026 to help residents learn about and use OpenGov, a tool that shows how the city spends its money. The council also discussed rules requiring people who lobby the city to register and report their activities.
keyTopics: OpenGov financial transparency tool - workshop to teach residents how to access city budget information
Lobbyist registration and reporting requirements under Cupertino Municipal Code Chapter 2.100
Community engagement on city spending and financial decisions

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 16, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The City Council meeting on April 16, 2026 included important legal disclosures about how residents can challenge city decisions in court. If you disagree with a city decision, you have 90 days from when that decision becomes final to file a lawsuit, according to state law.
keyTopics: 90-day deadline to sue the city over decisions
Legal challenges to city quasi-adjudicative decisions
California Code of Civil Procedure Section 1094.6 requirements
How to know when a city decision becomes 'final'

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met on April 7, 2026 to handle several administrative and planning matters. The main focus was reviewing a major update to the city's federal funding plan for 2025-2030, considering changes to how council vacancies are filled, and reviewing upcoming meeting schedules and agenda items.
keyTopics: Update to 5-year federal funding and development plan (2025-2030)
Change to council vacancy procedures
New workplan requests from individual council members
Approval of previous meeting minutes and upcoming meeting calendar

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 9, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council held a public hearing about a historic building at 405 Kipling Street in downtown Palo Alto. The owner is asking to upgrade the building's historic status, which would allow them to add 5,000 extra square feet of space for renovations and earthquake safety improvements.
keyTopics: 405 Kipling Street historic building upgrade request
5,000 sq ft bonus floor area for seismic and historic rehabilitation
Downtown Commercial zone development
Historic Resources Board meeting schedule approval
